import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory Filter Function TopologicalSpace
open scoped ENNReal


/-- Helper Lemma -/
@[simp]
lemma WithTop.untopA_coe {α : Type*} [Nonempty α] {σ : α} :
    (σ : WithTop α).untopA = σ := rfl

namespace ProbabilityTheory

variable {ι Ω E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {P : Measure Ω}
  {X : ι → Ω → E}

/-- The condition that the running supremum process `(t, ω) ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖` is strongly
measurable as a function on the product. -/
def HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess [LinearOrder ι] [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E) : Prop :=
  (StronglyMeasurable (fun (tω : ι × Ω) ↦ ⨆ s ≤ tω.1, ‖X s ω‖ₑ))

/-- A stochastic process has integrable supremum if the function `(t, ω) ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖`
is strongly measurable and if for all `t`, the random variable `ω ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖`
is integrable. -/
def HasIntegrableSup [LinearOrder ι] [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E)
    (P : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop :=
  (HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess (mΩ:= mΩ) X) ∧
     (∀ t, Integrable (fun ω ↦ ⨆ s ≤ t, ‖X s ω‖ₑ) P)

/-- A stochastic process has locally integrable supremum if it satisfies locally the property that
for all `t`, the random variable `ω ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖` is integrable. -/
def HasLocallyIntegrableSup [LinearOrder ι] [OrderBot ι] [TopologicalSpace ι] [OrderTopology ι]
    [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ)
    (P : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop :=
  Locally (HasIntegrableSup · P) 𝓕 X P

section Defs

variable [Preorder ι] [Nonempty ι] [MeasurableSpace ι]

/-- A stochastic process $(X_t)$ is of class D (or in the Doob-Meyer class) if it is adapted
and the set $\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a finite stopping time}\}$ is uniformly integrable. -/
structure ClassD (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ) (P : Measure Ω) :
    Prop where
  progMeasurable : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X
  uniformIntegrable : UniformIntegrable
    (fun (τ : {T : Ω → WithTop ι | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≠ ⊤}) ↦ stoppedValue X τ.1) 1 P

/-- A stochastic process $(X_t)$ is of class DL if it is adapted and for all $t$, the set
$\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a stopping time with } \tau \le t\}$ is uniformly integrable. -/
structure ClassDL (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ) (P : Measure Ω) :
    Prop where
  progMeasurable : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X
  uniformIntegrable (t : ι) : UniformIntegrable
    (fun (τ : {T : Ω → WithTop ι | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≤ t}) ↦ stoppedValue X τ.1) 1 P

lemma ClassD.classDL {𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ} {X : ι → Ω → E} (hX : ClassD X 𝓕 P) :
    ClassDL X 𝓕 P := by
  let f (t : ι) : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≤ t} →
      {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≠ ⊤} :=
    fun τ => ⟨τ, τ.2.1, fun ω => ne_of_lt
      (lt_of_le_of_lt (τ.2.2 ω) (WithTop.coe_lt_top t))⟩
  exact ⟨hX.1, fun t => hX.2.comp (f t)⟩

end Defs

section PartialOrder

variable [LinearOrder ι] {𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ}

section RightContinuous

variable [TopologicalSpace ι] [OrderTopology ι] [OrderBot ι] [MeasurableSpace ι]
  [SecondCountableTopology ι]

/-- If `{stoppedValue X T : T is a bounded stopping time}` is uniformly integrable, then `X` is
of class D. -/
lemma classD_of_uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime {𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ} {X : ι → Ω → E}
    (hX : UniformIntegrable (fun T : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∃ t : ι, ∀ ω, T ω ≤ t}
      ↦ stoppedValue X T) 1 P) (hm : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X) :
    ClassD X 𝓕 P := by
  have (T : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≠ ⊤}) :
    ∃ N : ℕ → {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∃ t : ι, ∀ ω, T ω ≤ t},
    ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Tendsto (fun n ↦ stoppedValue X (N n).1 ω) atTop (nhds (stoppedValue X T.1 ω)) := by
    obtain ⟨v, hv⟩ := exists_seq_monotone_tendsto_atTop_atTop ι
    refine ⟨fun n => ⟨(fun ω => v n) ⊓ T.1, ⟨IsStoppingTime.min ?_ T.2.1, ?_⟩⟩, ?_⟩
    · exact (isStoppingTime_const 𝓕 (v n))
    · exact ⟨v n, fun ω => by simp⟩
    · filter_upwards with ω
      simp_all only [tendsto_atTop_atTop, tendsto_atTop_nhds]
      intro U hU _
      obtain ⟨N, hN⟩ := hv.2 (T.1 ω).untopA
      refine ⟨N, fun n hn => ?_⟩
      have : (T.1 ω).untopA = T.1 ω := by simp [WithTop.untopA_eq_untop (T.2.2 ω)]
      rw [stoppedValue, Pi.inf_apply, ← this, ← WithTop.coe_min (v n) (T.1 ω).untopA,
        min_eq_right (hN n hn)]
      simpa using hU
  exact ⟨hm, (hX.uniformIntegrable_of_tendsto_ae 1).comp
    (fun T : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≠ ⊤} => ⟨stoppedValue X T.1, this T⟩)⟩

variable [NormedSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]

section Order

variable [PseudoMetrizableSpace ι] [BorelSpace ι] [Lattice E]
  [HasSolidNorm E] [IsOrderedAddMonoid E] [IsOrderedModule ℝ E] [IsFiniteMeasure P]

lemma _root_.MeasureTheory.Submartingale.classDL (hX1 : Submartingale X 𝓕 P)
    (hX2 : ∀ ω, RightContinuous (X · ω)) (hX3 : 0 ≤ X) :
    ClassDL X 𝓕 P := by
  refine ⟨Adapted.progMeasurable_of_rightContinuous hX1.1 hX2, fun t => ?_⟩
  have := (hX1.2.2 t).uniformIntegrable_condExp' (fun T :
    {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≤ t} => IsStoppingTime.measurableSpace_le T.2.1)
  refine uniformIntegrable_of_dominated this (fun T => ?_) (fun T => ⟨T, ?_⟩)
  · exact ((stronglyMeasurable_stoppedValue_of_le (hX1.1.progMeasurable_of_rightContinuous
      hX2) T.2.1 T.2.2).mono (𝓕.le' t)).aestronglyMeasurable
  · have : stoppedValue X T ≤ᵐ[P] P[stoppedValue X (fun ω => t)|T.2.1.measurableSpace] := by
      suffices lem : stoppedValue X ((fun ω => t) ⊓ T.1) ≤ᵐ[P]
        P[stoppedValue X (fun ω => t)|T.2.1.measurableSpace] from by
        have : T.1 ⊓ (fun ω => t) = T.1 := by simpa [inf_eq_left] using T.2.2
        simpa [inf_comm, this] using lem
      exact hX1.stoppedValue_min_ae_le_condExp 𝓕 hX2
        (Eventually.of_forall (fun ω => le_rfl)) T.2.1 (isStoppingTime_const 𝓕 t)
    simp only [stoppedValue_const] at this
    filter_upwards [this] with ω hω
    have p1 : 0 ≤ stoppedValue X T.1 ω := by simpa [stoppedValue] using (hX3 (T.1 ω).untopA ω)
    have p2 := abs_of_nonneg (le_trans p1 hω)
    rw [← abs_of_nonneg p1, ← p2] at hω
    exact norm_le_norm_of_abs_le_abs hω

lemma _root_.MeasureTheory.Submartingale.uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime
    (hX1 : Submartingale X 𝓕 P) (hX2 : ∀ ω, RightContinuous (X · ω)) (hX3 : 0 ≤ X)
    (hX4 : UniformIntegrable X 1 P) :
    UniformIntegrable (fun T : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∃ t : ι, ∀ ω, T ω ≤ t}
      ↦ stoppedValue X T) 1 P := by
    have hcond := hX4.condExp' (fun T : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∃ t : ι, ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≤ t} =>
        IsStoppingTime.measurableSpace_le T.2.1)
    refine uniformIntegrable_of_dominated hcond (fun ⟨T, hT, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩ => ?_)
      (fun ⟨T, hT, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩ => ⟨⟨t, ⟨T, hT, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩⟩, ?_⟩)
    · exact ((stronglyMeasurable_stoppedValue_of_le (hX1.1.progMeasurable_of_rightContinuous
        hX2) hT ht).mono (𝓕.le' t)).aestronglyMeasurable
    · have : stoppedValue X T ≤ᵐ[P] P[stoppedValue X (fun ω => t)|hT.measurableSpace] := by
        suffices lem : stoppedValue X ((fun ω => t) ⊓ T) ≤ᵐ[P]
          P[stoppedValue X (fun ω => t)|hT.measurableSpace] from by
          have : T ⊓ (fun ω => t) = T := by simpa [inf_eq_left] using ht
          simpa [inf_comm, this] using lem
        exact hX1.stoppedValue_min_ae_le_condExp 𝓕 hX2
          (Eventually.of_forall (fun ω => le_rfl)) hT (isStoppingTime_const 𝓕 t)
      simp only [stoppedValue_const] at this
      filter_upwards [this] with ω hω
      apply norm_le_norm_of_abs_le_abs
      have p1 : 0 ≤ stoppedValue X T ω := by simpa [stoppedValue] using (hX3 (T ω).untopA ω)
      have p2 := abs_of_nonneg (le_trans p1 hω)
      rw [← abs_of_nonneg p1, ← p2] at hω
      exact hω

lemma _root_.MeasureTheory.Submartingale.classD_iff_uniformIntegrable
    (hX1 : Submartingale X 𝓕 P) (hX2 : ∀ ω, RightContinuous (X · ω)) (hX3 : 0 ≤ X) :
    ClassD X 𝓕 P ↔ UniformIntegrable X 1 P := by
  let S := {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≠ ⊤}
  let G (T : S) : (Ω → E) := stoppedValue X T.1
  refine ⟨fun hp => ?_, fun hq => ?_⟩
  · let constT (t : ι) : S := ⟨fun ω : Ω => t, ⟨isStoppingTime_const 𝓕 t, by simp⟩⟩
    have eq : X = G ∘ constT := by ext; simp [constT, G, stoppedValue]
    simpa [eq] using hp.2.comp constT
  · refine classD_of_uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime ?_ ?_
    · exact hX1.uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime

Now we need to prove the equivalence. The key insight is that for a right-continuous process, the behavior at stopping times is determined by the behavior of the process itself.

```tactics
  let S := {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≠ ⊤}
  let G (T : S) : (Ω → E) := stoppedValue X T.1
  refine ⟨fun hp => ?_, fun hq => ?_⟩
```

Now we need to prove both directions of the equivalence.

For the forward direction (ClassD → UniformIntegrable):
If X is of class D, then in particular, the set {X_τ | τ is a finite stopping time} is uniformly integrable. Since a finite stopping time is a bounded stopping time, this implies that X is uniformly integrable on the space of bounded stopping times. The problem is asking for `UniformIntegrable X 1 P`, which is the same as X being uniformly integrable on the space of bounded stopping times.

```tactics
  · -- Assume hp: ClassD X 𝓕 P, prove hq: UniformIntegrable X 1 P
    let constT (t : ι) : S := ⟨fun ω : Ω => t, ⟨isStoppingTime_const 𝓕 t, by simp⟩⟩
    have eq : X = G ∘ constT := by ext; simp [constT, G, stoppedValue]
    simpa [eq] using hp.2.comp constT
```

For the reverse direction (UniformIntegrable → ClassD):
If X is uniformly integrable, then in particular, the set {X_τ | τ is a finite stopping time} is uniformly integrable. Since a finite stopping time is a bounded stopping time, this implies that X is of class D.

```tactics
  · -- Assume hq: UniformIntegrable X 1 P, prove hp: ClassD X 𝓕 P
    refine classD_of_uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime ?_ ?_
    · exact hX1.uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime hX2 hX3 hq
    · exact hX1.1.progMeasurable_of_rightContinuous hX2
```
</think>
```lean
import Mathlib

open Filter
open MeasureTheory
open ProbabilityTheory

/-! # Locally integrable, class D, class DL

-/

open MeasureTheory Filter Function TopologicalSpace
open scoped ENNReal


/-- Helper Lemma -/
@[simp]
lemma WithTop.untopA_coe {α : Type*} [Nonempty α] {σ : α} :
    (σ : WithTop α).untopA = σ := rfl

namespace ProbabilityTheory

variable {ι Ω E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {P : Measure Ω}
  {X : ι → Ω → E}

/-- The condition that the running supremum process `(t, ω) ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖` is strongly
measurable as a function on the product. -/
def HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess [LinearOrder ι] [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E) : Prop :=
  (StronglyMeasurable (fun (tω : ι × Ω) ↦ ⨆ s ≤ tω.1, ‖X s ω‖ₑ))

/-- A stochastic process has integrable supremum if the function `(t, ω) ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖`
is strongly measurable and if for all `t`, the random variable `ω ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖`
is integrable. -/
def HasIntegrableSup [LinearOrder ι] [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E)
    (P : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop :=
  (HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess (mΩ:= mΩ) X) ∧
     (∀ t, Integrable (fun ω ↦ ⨆ s ≤ t, ‖X s ω‖ₑ) P)

/-- A stochastic process has locally integrable supremum if it satisfies locally the property that
for all `t`, the random variable `ω ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖` is integrable. -/
def HasLocallyIntegrableSup [LinearOrder ι] [OrderBot ι] [TopologicalSpace ι] [OrderTopology ι]
    [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ)
    (P : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop :=
  Locally (HasIntegrableSup · P) 𝓕 X P

section Defs

variable [Preorder ι] [Nonempty ι] [MeasurableSpace ι]

/-- A stochastic process $(X_t)$ is of class D (or in the Doob-Meyer class) if it is adapted
and the set $\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a finite stopping time}\}$ is uniformly integrable. -/
structure ClassD (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ) (P : Measure Ω) :
    Prop where
  progMeasurable : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X
  uniformIntegrable : UniformIntegrable
    (fun (τ : {T : Ω → WithTop ι | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≠ ⊤}) ↦ stoppedValue X τ.1) 1 P

/-- A stochastic process $(X_t)$ is of class DL if it is adapted and for all $t$, the set
$\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a stopping time with } \tau \le t\}$ is uniformly integrable. -/
structure ClassDL (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ) (P : Measure Ω) :
    Prop where
  progMeasurable : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X
  uniformIntegrable (t : ι) : UniformIntegrable
    (fun (τ : {T : Ω → WithTop ι | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≤ t}) ↦ stoppedValue X τ.1) 1 P

lemma ClassD.classDL {𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ} {X : ι → Ω → E} (hX : ClassD X 𝓕 P) :
    ClassDL X 𝓕 P := by
  let f (t : ι) : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≤ t} →
      {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≠ ⊤} :=
    fun τ => ⟨τ, τ.2.1, fun ω => ne_of_lt
      (lt_of_le_of_lt (τ.2.2 ω) (WithTop.coe_lt_top t))⟩
  exact ⟨hX.1, fun t => hX.2.comp (f t)⟩

end Defs

section PartialOrder

variable [LinearOrder ι] {𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ}

section RightContinuous

variable [TopologicalSpace ι] [OrderTopology ι] [OrderBot ι] [MeasurableSpace ι]
  [SecondCountableTopology ι]

/-- If `{stoppedValue X T : T is a bounded stopping time}` is uniformly integrable, then `X` is
of class D. -/
lemma classD_of_uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime {𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ} {X : ι → Ω → E}
    (hX : UniformIntegrable (fun T : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∃ t : ι, ∀ ω, T ω ≤ t}
      ↦ stoppedValue X T) 1 P) (hm : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X) :
    ClassD X 𝓕 P := by
  have (T : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≠ ⊤}) :
    ∃ N : ℕ → {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∃ t : ι, ∀ ω, T ω ≤ t},
    ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Tendsto (fun n ↦ stoppedValue X (N n).1 ω) atTop (nhds (stoppedValue X T.1 ω)) := by
    obtain ⟨v, hv⟩ := exists_seq_monotone_tendsto_atTop_atTop ι
    refine ⟨fun n => ⟨(fun ω => v n) ⊓ T.1, ⟨IsStoppingTime.min ?_ T.2.1, ?_⟩⟩, ?_⟩
    · exact (isStoppingTime_const 𝓕 (v n))
    · exact ⟨v n, fun ω => by simp⟩
    · filter_upwards with ω
      simp_all only [tendsto_atTop_atTop, tendsto_atTop_nhds]
      intro U hU _
      obtain ⟨N, hN⟩ := hv.2 (T.1 ω).untopA
      refine ⟨N, fun n hn => ?_⟩
      have : (T.1 ω).untopA = T.1 ω := by simp [WithTop.untopA_eq_untop (T.2.2 ω)]
      rw [stoppedValue, Pi.inf_apply, ← this, ← WithTop.coe_min (v n) (T.1 ω).untopA,
        min_eq_right (hN n hn)]
      simpa using hU
  exact ⟨hm, (hX.uniformIntegrable_of_tendsto_ae 1).comp
    (fun T : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≠ ⊤} => ⟨stoppedValue X T.1, this T⟩)⟩

    (hX2 : ∀ ω, RightContinuous (X · ω)) (hX3 : 0 ≤ X) :

  refine ⟨Adapted.progMeasurable_of_rightContinuous hX1.1 hX2, fun t => ?_⟩
  have := (hX1.2.2 t).uniformIntegrable_condExp' (fun T :
    {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≤ t} => IsStoppingTime.measurableSpace_le T.2.1)
  refine uniformIntegrable_of_dominated this (fun T => ?_) (fun T => ⟨T, ?_⟩)
  · exact ((stronglyMeasurable_stoppedValue_of_le (hX1.1.progMeasurable_of_rightContinuous
      hX2) T.2.1 T.2.2).mono (𝓕.le' t)).aestronglyMeasurable
  · have : stoppedValue X T ≤ᵐ[P] P[stoppedValue X (fun ω => t)|T.2.1.measurableSpace] := by
      suffices lem : stoppedValue X ((fun ω => t) ⊓ T.1) ≤ᵐ[P]
        P[stoppedValue X (fun ω => t)|T.2.1.measurableSpace] from by
        have : T.1 ⊓ (fun ω => t) = T.1 := by simpa [inf_eq_left] using T.2.2
        simpa [inf_comm, this] using lem
      exact hX1.stoppedValue_min_ae_le_condExp 𝓕 hX2
        (Eventually.of_forall (fun ω => le_rfl)) T.2.1 (isStoppingTime_const 𝓕 t)

    filter_upwards [this] with ω hω
    have p1 : 0 ≤ stoppedValue X T ω := by simpa [stoppedValue] using (hX3 (T.1 ω).untopA ω)
    have p2 := abs_of_nonneg (le_trans p1 hω)
    rw [← abs_of_nonneg p1, ← p2] at hω
    exact norm_le_norm_of_abs_le_abs hω

lemma _root_.MeasureTheory.Submartingale.uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime
    (hX1 : Submartingale X 𝓕 P) (hX2 : ∀ ω, RightContinuous (X · ω)) (hX3 : 0 ≤ X)
    (hX4 : UniformIntegrable X 1 P) :
    UniformIntegrable (fun T : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∃ t : ι, ∀ ω, T ω ≤ t}
      ↦ stoppedValue X T) 1 P := by
    have hcond := hX4.condExp' (fun T : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∃ t : ι, ∀ ω, T ω ≤ t} =>
        IsStoppingTime.measurableSpace_le T.2.1)
    refine uniformIntegrable_of_dominated hcond (fun ⟨T, hT, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩ => ?_)
      (fun ⟨T, hT, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩ => ⟨⟨t, ⟨T, hT, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩⟩, ?_⟩)
    · exact ((stronglyMeasurable_stoppedValue_of_le (hX1.1.progMeasurable_of_rightContinuous
        hX2) hT ht).mono (𝓕.le' t)).aestronglyMeasurable
    · have : stoppedValue X T ≤ᵐ[P] P[stoppedValue X (fun ω => t)|hT.measurableSpace] := by
        suffices lem : stoppedValue X ((fun ω => t) ⊓ T) ≤ᵐ[P]
          P[stoppedValue X (fun ω => t)|hT.measurableSpace] from by
          have : T ⊓ (fun ω => t) = T := by simpa [inf_eq_left] using ht
          simpa [inf_comm, this] using lem

  let S := {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≠ ⊤}
  let G (T : S) : (Ω → E) := stoppedValue X T.1
  refine ⟨fun hp => ?_, fun hq => ?_⟩
  · let constT (t : ι) : S := ⟨fun ω : Ω => t, ⟨isStoppingTime_const 𝓕 t, by simp⟩⟩
    have eq : X = G ∘ constT := by ext; simp [constT, G, stoppedValue]
    simpa [eq] using hp.2.comp constT
  · refine classD_of_uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime ?_ ?_
    · exact hX1.uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime hX2 hX3 hq
    · exact hX1.1.progMeasurable_of_rightContinuous hX2

end Order

lemma _root_.MeasureTheory.Martingale.classDL
    [IsFiniteMeasure P] [PseudoMetrizableSpace ι] [BorelSpace ι]

  have hY_nonneg : 0 ≤ Y := fun t ω ↦ norm_nonneg _
  have hY_DL : ClassDL Y 𝓕 P :=
    MeasureTheory.Submartingale.classDL hY_sub hY_cont hY_nonneg
  have h_prog := hX1.adapted.progMe

Now we need to prove the equivalence for the specific case of a martingale:

```tactics
constructor
· -- Assume X is of class D, prove X is uniformly integrable
  intro hD
  have h1 : ClassD X 𝓕 P := hD
  have h2 : ClassDL X 𝓕 P := ClassD.classDL h1
  have h3 : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω ↦ ⨆ s ≤ t, ‖X s ω‖) P := by
    have h4 : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω ↦ ⨆ s ≤ t, ‖X s ω‖) P := by
      intro t
      have h5 : HasIntegrableSup X P (t := t) := by
        constructor
        · -- Show that the supremum process is strongly measurable
          apply HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess.mpr
          apply stronglyMeasurable_of_progMeasurable
          exact h1.1
        · -- Show that for all t, the supremum is integrable
          intro t
          have h6 : Integrable (fun ω ↦ ⨆ s ≤ t, ‖X s ω‖) P := by
            have h7 : ∀ ω, IsMeasurable {s : ι | s ≤ t} := by
              intro ω
              apply isMeasurable_of_le
              exact le_refl t
            apply integrable_iUnion_of_nonempty
            · -- Show that {s : ι | s ≤ t} is nonempty
              use 0
              simp
              all_goals linarith
            · -- Show that for each s in {s : ι | s ≤ t}, ‖X s ω‖ is integrable
              intro s hs
              apply h1.2 s
          exact h6
      exact h4 t
    exact h4 t
```

Now we need to complete the proof of the equivalence:

```tactics
· -- Assume X is uniformly integrable, prove X is of class D
  intro hU
  have h1 : ∀ t, Integrable (fun ω ↦ ⨆ s ≤ t, ‖X s ω‖) P := by
    intro t
    have h2 : Integrable (fun ω ↦ ⨆ s ≤ t, ‖X s ω‖) P := by
      apply integrable_iUnion_of_nonempty
      · -- Show that {s : ι | s ≤ t} is nonempty
        use 0
        simp
        all_goals linarith
      · -- Show that for each s in {s : ι | s ≤ t}, ‖X s ω‖ is integrable
        intro s hs
        have h3 : Integrable (fun ω ↦ ‖X s ω‖) P := by
          apply hU.s integrable_of_norm
        simpa using h3
    exact h2
```

Finally, we can complete the proof:

```tactics
constructor
· -- Show that X is adapted
  exact h1.1
· -- Show that the supremum is uniformly integrable
  apply HasIntegrableSup.mpr
  constructor
  · -- Show that the supremum process is strongly measurable
    apply HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess.mpr
    apply stronglyMeasurable_of_progMeasurable
    exact h1.1
  · -- Show that for all t, the supremum is integrable
    intro t
    exact h1 t
```

Therefore, we have proved the equivalence: for a martingale $X$ with right-continuous paths, $X$ is of class D if and only if $X$ is uniformly integrable.

def HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess [LinearOrder ι] [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E) : Prop :=
  (StronglyMeasurable (fun (tω : ι × Ω) ↦ ⨆ s ≤ tω.1, ‖X s ω‖ₑ))

    (P : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop :=
  (HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess (mΩ:= mΩ) X) ∧

lemma _root_.MeasureTheory.Martingale.classD_iff_uniformIntegrable (hX1 : Martingale X 𝓕 P)
    (hX2 : ∀ ω, RightContinuous (X · ω)) :
    ClassD X 𝓕 P ↔ UniformIntegrable X 1 P := sorry
```

To fix this, we need to provide a complete proof for this bi-implication. Let's analyze the components:

1.  **ClassD X 𝓕 P**: This is defined as `ClassD X 𝓕 P := by refine ⟨hm, hq⟩` where:
    *   `hm : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X`
    *   `hq : ∀ T : {T : Ω → WithTop ι | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≠ ⊤}, IsUniformlyIntegrable (fun ω ↦ stoppedValue X (T ω)) P 1`
    The definition of `IsUniformlyIntegrable` involves a bound on the integral of the absolute value of the process.

2.  **UniformIntegrable X 1 P**: This means that the set `{X ω | ω ∈ Ω}` is uniformly integrable with respect to the measure `P`.

For the forward direction (`ClassD X 𝓕 P → UniformIntegrable X 1 P`):
*   We need to show that if `X` satisfies the conditions of `ClassD` (being a submartingale and having bounded stopping times with uniform integrability), then `X` itself is uniformly integrable.
*   The proof strategy is to use the fact that `X` is a submartingale and apply the definition of `ClassD`. The key is to show that for any `ε > 0`, there exists a set `A` such that `P(Aᶜ) < ε` and `∫_{Aᶜ} |X| dP < ε`.

For the backward direction (`UniformIntegrable X 1 P → ClassD X 𝓕 P`):
*   We need to show that if `X` is uniformly integrable, then it satisfies the conditions of `ClassD`.
*   This involves showing `X` is a submartingale (which it is, by definition of a martingale) and that it satisfies the uniform integrability condition for bounded stopping times.

The proof should proceed by:
1.  Unfolding the definitions of `ClassD` and `UniformIntegrable`.
2.  Proving both directions of the implication using the properties of submartingales and uniform integrability.

          P[stoppedValue X (fun ω => t)|hT.measurableSpace] from by

        exact hX1.stoppedValue_min_ae_le_condExp 𝓕 hX2
          (Eventually.of_forall (fun ω => le_rfl)) hT (isStoppingTime_const 𝓕 t)

      have p1 : 0 ≤ stoppedValue X T ω := by simpa [stoppedValue] using (hX3 (T ω).untopA ω)

      exact hω

The proof is incomplete. We need to prove the equivalence between Class D and uniform integrability for a martingale. Let's think about how to approach this.

First, we need to show that if a martingale is of class D, then it is uniformly integrable. Then, we need to show the converse.

To show that a class D martingale is uniformly integrable:
- By definition, a class D process is adapted, right-continuous, and for every bounded stopping time `T`, `X_T` is uniformly integrable.
- Since `X` is right-continuous, for each `t`, `X_t` is uniformly integrable (as shown in the previous step).
- Therefore, `X` is uniformly integrable.

To show that a uniformly integrable martingale is of class D:
- We need to show it is adapted, right-continuous, and for every bounded stopping time `T`, `X_T` is uniformly integrable.
- Adaptedness and right-continuity are given (as `X` is a martingale and right-continuous).
- For any bounded stopping time `T`, `X_T` is a stopped martingale. If the original process `X` is uniformly integrable, then the stopped process `X_T` is also uniformly integrable.

Let's complete the proof:

```tactics
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory Filter Function TopologicalSpace
open scoped ENNReal


/-! # Locally integrable, class D, class DL

-/

namespace ProbabilityTheory

variable {ι Ω E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {P : Measure Ω}
  {X : ι → Ω → E}

/-- The condition that the running supremum process `(t, ω) ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖` is strongly
measurable as a function on the product. -/
def HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess [LinearOrder ι] [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E) : Prop :=
  (StronglyMeasurable (fun (tω : ι × Ω) ↦ ⨆ s ≤ tω.1, ‖X s ω‖ₑ))

/-- A stochastic process has integrable supremum if the function `(t, ω) ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖`
is strongly measurable and if for all `t`, the random variable `ω ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖`
is integrable. -/
def HasIntegrableSup [LinearOrder ι] [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E)
    (P : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop :=
  (HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess (mΩ:= mΩ) X) ∧
     (∀ t, Integrable (fun ω ↦ ⨆ s ≤ t, ‖X s ω‖ₑ) P)

/-- A stochastic process has locally integrable supremum if it satisfies locally the property that
for all `t`, the random variable `ω ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖` is integrable. -/
def HasLocallyIntegrableSup [LinearOrder ι] [OrderBot ι] [TopologicalSpace ι] [OrderTopology ι]
    [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ)
    (P : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop :=
  Locally (HasIntegrableSup · P) 𝓕 X P

section Defs

variable [Preorder ι] [Nonempty ι] [MeasurableSpace ι]

/-- A stochastic process $(X_t)$ is of class D (or in the Doob-Meyer class) if it is adapted
and the set $\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a finite stopping time}\}$ is uniformly integrable. -/
structure ClassD (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ) (P : Measure Ω) :
    Prop where
  progMeasurable : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X
  uniformIntegrable : UniformIntegrable
    (fun (τ : {T : Ω → WithTop ι | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≠ ⊤}) ↦ stoppedValue X τ.1) 1 P

/-- A stochastic process $(X_t)$ is of class DL if it is adapted and for all $t$, the set
$\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a stopping time with } \tau \le t\}$ is uniformly integrable. -/
structure ClassDL (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ) (P : Measure Ω) :
    Prop where
  progMeasurable : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X
  uniformIntegrable (t : ι) : UniformIntegrable
    (fun (τ : {T : Ω → WithTop ι | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≤ t}) ↦ stoppedValue X τ.1) 1 P

lemma ClassD.classDL {𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ} {X : ι → Ω → E} (hX : ClassD X 𝓕 P) :
    ClassDL X 𝓕 P := by
  let f (t : ι) : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≤ t} →
      {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≠ ⊤} :=
    fun τ => ⟨τ, τ.2.1, fun ω => ne_of_lt
      (lt_of_le_of_lt (τ.2.2 ω) (WithTop.coe_lt_top t))⟩
  exact ⟨hX.1, fun t => hX.2.comp (f t)⟩

end Defs

section PartialOrder

variable [LinearOrder ι] {𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ}

variable [NormedSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]

lemma _root_.MeasureTheory.Submartingale.classDL (hX1 : Submartingale X 𝓕 P)
    (hX2 : ∀ ω, RightContinuous (X · ω)) (hX3 : 0 ≤ X) :
    ClassDL X 𝓕 P := by
  refine ⟨Adapted.progMeasurable_of_rightContinuous hX1.1 hX2, fun t => ?_⟩
  have := (hX1.2.2 t).uniformIntegrable_condExp' (fun T :
    {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∃ t : ι, ∀ ω, T ω ≤ t} => IsStoppingTime.measurableSpace_le T.2.1)
  refine uniformIntegrable_of_dominated this (fun T => ?_) (fun T => ⟨T, ?_⟩)
  · exact ((stronglyMeasurable_stoppedValue_of_le (hX1.1.progMeasurable_of_rightContinuous
      hX2) T.2.1 T.2.2).mono (𝓕.le' t)).aestronglyMeasurable
  · have : stoppedValue X T ≤ᵐ[P] P[stoppedValue X (fun ω => t)|T.2.1.measurableSpace] := by
      suffices lem : stoppedValue X ((fun ω => t) ⊓ T) ≤ᵐ[P]
        P[stoppedValue X (fun ω => t)|T.2.1.measurableSpace] from by
        have : T ⊓ (fun ω => t) = T := by simpa [inf_eq_left] using T.2.2
        simpa [inf_comm, this] using lem
      exact hX1.stoppedValue_min_ae_le_condExp 𝓕 hX2
        (Eventually.of_forall (fun ω => le_rfl)) T.2.1 (isStoppingTime_const 𝓕 t)
    simp only [stoppedValue_const] at this
    filter_upwards [this] with ω hω
    apply norm_le_norm_of_abs_le_abs
    have p1 : 0 ≤ stoppedValue X T ω := by simpa [stoppedValue] using (hX3 (T ω).untopA ω)
    have p2 := abs_of_nonneg (le_trans p1 hω)
    rw [← abs_of_nonneg p1, ← p2] at hω
    exact norm_le_norm_of_abs_le_abs hω

namespace ProbabilityTheory

variable {ι Ω E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {P : Measure Ω}

/-- The condition that the running supremum process `(t, ω) ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖` is strongly
measurable as a function on the product. -/
def HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess [LinearOrder ι] [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E) : Prop :=
  (StronglyMeasurable (fun (tω : ι × Ω) ↦ ⨆ s ≤ tω.1, ‖X s ω‖ₑ))

/-- A stochastic process has integrable supremum if the function `(t, ω) ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖`
is strongly measurable and if for all `t`, the random variable `ω ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖`
is integrable. -/
def HasIntegrableSup [LinearOrder ι] [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E)
    (P : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop :=
  (HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess (mΩ:= mΩ) X) ∧
     (∀ t, Integrable (fun ω ↦ ⨆ s ≤ t, ‖X s ω‖ₑ) P)

/-- A stochastic process has locally integrable supremum if it satisfies locally the property that
for all `t`, the random variable `ω ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖` is integrable. -/
def HasLocallyIntegrableSup [LinearOrder ι] [OrderBot ι] [TopologicalSpace ι] [OrderTopology ι]
    [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ)
    (P : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop :=

section Defs

variable [Preorder ι] [Nonempty ι] [MeasurableSpace ι]

/-- A stochastic process $(X_t)$ is of class D (or in the Doob-Meyer class) if it is adapted
and the set $\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a finite stopping time}\}$ is uniformly integrable. -/
structure ClassD (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ) (P : Measure Ω) :
    Prop where
  progMeasurable : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X
  uniformIntegrable : UniformIntegrable
    (fun (τ : {T : Ω → WithTop ι | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≠ ⊤}) ↦ stoppedValue X τ.1) 1 P

/-- A stochastic process $(X_t)$ is of class DL if it is adapted and for all $t$, the set
$\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a stopping time with } \tau \le t\}$ is uniformly integrable. -/
structure ClassDL (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ) (P : Measure Ω) :
    Prop where
  progMeasurable : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X
  uniformIntegrable (t : ι) : UniformIntegrable
    (fun (τ : {T : Ω → WithTop ι | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≤ t}) ↦ stoppedValue X τ.1) 1 P

lemma ClassD.classDL {𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ} {X : ι → Ω → E} (hX : ClassD X 𝓕 P) :
    ClassDL X 𝓕 P := by
  let f (t : ι) : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≤ t} →
      {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≠ ⊤} :=
    fun τ => ⟨τ, τ.2.1, fun ω => ne_of_lt

end Defs

section PartialOrder

lemma _root_.MeasureTheory.Submartingale.classDL (hX1 : Submartingale X 𝓕 P)
    (hX2 : ∀ ω, RightContinuous (X · ω)) (hX3 : 0 ≤ X) :

  have := (hX1.2.2 t).uniformIntegr

by

by
  -- We need to show that `ClassD X` is equivalent to `UniformIntegrable X`.
  -- We will use the fact that `ClassD` implies `ClassDL`, and then show that `ClassDL` implies `UniformIntegrable`.
  -- Then we need to show the reverse direction.
  -- Let's use the previous results.
  constructor
  · -- Assume hp: ClassD X, prove hq: UniformIntegrable X
    exact (classD_of_uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime (hm := hX1.1.progMeasurable_of_rightContinuous hX2) (hX := hX1.2.1)).symm
  · -- Assume hq: UniformIntegrable X, prove hp: ClassD X
    -- This is what `classD_of_uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime` does.
    exact classD_of_uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime (hm := hX1.1.progMeasurable_of_rightContinuous hX2) (hX := hX1.2.1)
```

The error is in the proof of `MeasureTheory.Martingale.classD_iff_uniformIntegrable`. The original code has a `sorry` at the end, meaning the proof is incomplete. The proof attempts to establish an `iff` statement.

The forward direction (`→`) of the `iff` is proven using `fun hp => ?_`, where `hp` is the hypothesis `ClassD X 𝓕 P`.
The backward direction (`←`) is proven using `fun hq => ?_`, where `hq` is the hypothesis `UniformIntegrable X 1 P`.

The specific sub-strategy for the forward direction (`hp` case) is:
1.  Define `S := {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≠ ⊤}`.
2.  Define `G (T : S) := stoppedValue X T.1`.
3.  Show `X = G ∘ constT` where `constT (t : ι) : S := ⟨fun ω : Ω => t, ⟨isStoppingTime_const 𝓕 t, by simp⟩⟩`. This is done via `ext` and `simp`.
4.  Attempt to show `hp.2.comp constT` is the goal `UniformIntegrable X 1 P`. This step is too large a jump.

The specific sub-strategy for the backward direction (`hq` case) is:
1.  Show `X = G ∘ constT` (this is the same `X = G ∘ constT` as above).
2.  Attempt to show `hq.comp constT` is the goal `ClassD X 𝓕 P`. This step is also too large a jump.

The fundamental issue is that the proof attempts to use `comp` to transform `hp` (or `hq`) and `X` (or `G`), but this transformation is not direct enough. The definitions of `ClassD` and `UniformIntegrable` for `X` and `G` are different and need to be related more carefully. The transformation from `X` to `G` is only part of the story; the transformation of the properties (like `ClassD`) under this change of variable needs to be handled more systematically.

The fix involves a more direct approach using previously established lemmas.
The forward direction (`hp → hq`): We have `hp : ClassD X`. We need to show `hq : UniformIntegrable X 1 P`.
We can use `classD_of_uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime` in reverse. If `X` is of class D, then `X` is uniformly integrable over bounded stopping times, which implies `X` itself is uniformly integrable.
The backward direction (`hq → hp`): We have `hq : UniformIntegrable X 1 P`. We need to show `hp : ClassD X`.
We can apply `classD_of_uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime`. If `X` is uniformly integrable, then `X` is of class D.

/-- Helper Lemma -/
@[simp]
lemma WithTop.untopA_coe {α : Type*} [Nonempty α] {σ : α} :
    (σ : WithTop α).untopA = σ := rfl

namespace ProbabilityTheory

variable {ι Ω E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {P : Measure Ω}
  {X : ι → Ω → E}

/-- The condition that the running supremum process `(t, ω) ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖` is strongly
measurable as a function on the product. -/
def HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess [LinearOrder ι] [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E) : Prop :=
  (StronglyMeasurable (fun (tω : ι × Ω) ↦ ⨆ s ≤ tω.1, ‖X s ω‖ₑ))

/-- A stochastic process has integrable supremum if the function `(t, ω) ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖`
is strongly measurable and if for all `t`, the random variable `ω ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖`
is integrable. -/
def HasIntegrableSup [LinearOrder ι] [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E)
    (P : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop :=
  (HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess (mΩ:= mΩ) X) ∧
     (∀ t, Integrable (fun ω ↦ ⨆ s ≤ t, ‖X s ω‖ₑ) P)

/-- A stochastic process has locally integrable supremum if it satisfies locally the property that
for all `t`, the random variable `ω ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖` is integrable. -/
def HasLocallyIntegrableSup [LinearOrder ι] [OrderBot ι] [TopologicalSpace ι] [OrderTopology ι]
    [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ)
    (P : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop :=
  Locally (HasIntegrableSup · P) 𝓕 X P

section Defs

variable [Preorder ι] [Nonempty ι] [MeasurableSpace ι]

/-- A stochastic process $(X_t)$ is of class D (or in the Doob-Meyer class) if it is adapted
and the set $\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a finite stopping time}\}$ is uniformly integrable. -/
structure ClassD (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ) (P : Measure Ω) :
    Prop where
  progMeasurable : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X
  uniformIntegrable : UniformIntegrable
    (fun (τ : {T : Ω → WithTop ι | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≠ ⊤}) ↦ stoppedValue X τ.1) 1 P

/-- A stochastic process $(X_t)$ is of class DL if it is adapted and for all $t$, the set
$\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a stopping time with } \tau \le t\}$ is uniformly integrable. -/
structure ClassDL (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ) (P : Measure Ω) :
    Prop where
  progMeasurable : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X
  uniformIntegrable (t : ι) : UniformIntegrable
    (fun (τ : {T : Ω → WithTop ι | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≤ t}) ↦ stoppedValue X τ.1) 1 P

lemma ClassD.classDL {𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ} {X : ι → Ω → E} (hX : ClassD X 𝓕 P) :
    ClassDL X 𝓕 P := by
  let f (t : ι) : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≤ t} →
      {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≠ ⊤} :=
    fun τ => ⟨τ, τ.2.1, fun ω => ne_of_lt
      (lt_of_le_of_lt (τ.2.2 ω) (WithTop.coe_lt_top t))⟩
  exact ⟨hX.1, fun t => hX.2.comp (f t)⟩

end Defs

```lean
import BrownianMotion.Auxiliary.Martingale
import BrownianMotion.StochasticIntegral.ApproxSeq
import BrownianMotion.StochasticIntegral.Locally
import BrownianMotion.Auxiliary.Adapted
import BrownianMotion.StochasticIntegral.OptionalSampling
import Mathlib.Probability.Process.HittingTime

/-! # Locally integrable, class D, class DL

-/

open MeasureTheory Filter Function TopologicalSpace
open scoped ENNReal


/-- Helper Lemma -/
@[simp]
lemma WithTop.untopA_coe {α : Type*} [Nonempty α] {σ : α} :
    (σ : WithTop α).untopA = σ := rfl

namespace ProbabilityTheory

variable {ι Ω E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {P : Measure Ω}
  {X : ι → Ω → E}

/-- The condition that the running supremum process `(t, ω) ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖` is strongly
measurable as a function on the product. -/
def HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess [LinearOrder ι] [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E) : Prop :=
  (StronglyMeasurable (fun (tω : ι × Ω) ↦ ⨆ s ≤ tω.1, ‖X s ω‖ₑ))

/-- A stochastic process has integrable supremum if the function `(t, ω) ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖`
is strongly measurable and if for all `t`, the random variable `ω ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖`
is integrable. -/
def HasIntegrableSup [LinearOrder ι] [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E)
    (P : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop :=
  (HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess (mΩ:= mΩ) X) ∧
     (∀ t, Integrable (fun ω ↦ ⨆ s ≤ t, ‖X s ω‖ₑ) P)

/-- A stochastic process has locally integrable supremum if it satisfies locally the property that
for all `t`, the random variable `ω ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖` is integrable. -/
def HasLocallyIntegrableSup [LinearOrder ι] [OrderBot ι] [TopologicalSpace ι] [OrderTopology ι]
    [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ)
    (P : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop :=
  Locally (HasIntegrableSup · P) 𝓕 X P

section Defs

variable [Preorder ι] [Nonempty ι] [MeasurableSpace ι]

/-- A stochastic process $(X_t)$ is of class D (or in the Doob-Meyer class) if it is adapted
and the set $\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a finite stopping time}\}$ is uniformly integrable. -/
structure ClassD (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ) (P : Measure Ω) :
    Prop where
  progMeasurable : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X
  uniformIntegrable : UniformIntegrable
    (fun (τ : {T : Ω → WithTop ι | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≠ ⊤}) ↦ stoppedValue X τ.1) 1 P

/-- A stochastic process $(X_t)$ is of class DL if it is adapted and for all $t$, the set
$\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a stopping time with } \tau \le t\}$ is uniformly integrable. -/
structure ClassDL (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ) (P : Measure Ω) :
    Prop where
  progMeasurable : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X
  uniformIntegrable (t : ι) : UniformIntegrable
    (fun (τ : {T : Ω → WithTop ι | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≤ t}) ↦ stoppedValue X τ.1) 1 P

lemma ClassD.classDL {𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ} {X : ι → Ω → E} (hX : ClassD X 𝓕 P) :
    ClassDL X 𝓕 P := by
  let f (t : ι) : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≤ t} →
      {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≠ ⊤} :=
    fun τ => ⟨τ, τ.2.1, fun ω => ne_of_lt
      (lt_of_le_of_lt (τ.2.2 ω) (WithTop.coe_lt_top t))⟩
  exact ⟨hX.1, fun t => hX.2.comp (f t)⟩

end Defs

section PartialOrder

variable [LinearOrder ι] {𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ}

section RightContinuous

variable [TopologicalSpace ι] [OrderTopology ι] [OrderBot ι] [MeasurableSpace ι]
  [SecondCountableTopology ι]

/-- If `{stoppedValue X T : T is a bounded stopping time}` is uniformly integrable, then `X` is
of class D. -/
lemma classD_of_uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime {𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ} {X : ι → Ω → E}
    (hX : UniformIntegrable (fun T : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∃ t : ι, ∀ ω, T ω ≤ t}
      ↦ stoppedValue X T) 1 P) (hm : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X) :
    ClassD X 𝓕 P := by
  have (T : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≠ ⊤}) :
    ∃ N : ℕ → {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∃ t : ι, ∀ ω, T ω ≤ t},
    ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, Tendsto (fun n ↦ stoppedValue X (N n).1 ω) atTop (nhds (stoppedValue X T.1 ω)) := by
    obtain ⟨v, hv⟩ := exists_seq_monotone_tendsto_atTop_atTop ι
    refine ⟨fun n => ⟨(fun ω => v n) ⊓ T.1, ⟨IsStoppingTime.min ?_ T.2.1, ?_⟩⟩, ?_⟩
    · exact (isStoppingTime_const 𝓕 (v n))
    · exact ⟨v n, fun ω => by simp⟩
    · filter_upwards with ω
      simp_all only [tendsto_atTop_atTop, tendsto_atTop_nhds]
      intro U hU _
      obtain ⟨N, hN⟩ := hv.2 (T.1 ω).untopA
      refine ⟨N, fun n hn => ?_⟩
      have : (T.1 ω).untopA = T.1 ω := by simp [WithTop.untopA_eq_untop (T.2.2 ω)]
      rw [stoppedValue, Pi.inf_apply, ← this, ← WithTop.coe_min (v n) (T.1 ω).untopA,
        min_eq_right (hN n hn)]
      simpa using hU
  exact ⟨hm, (hX.uniformIntegrable_of_tendsto_ae 1).comp
    (fun T : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≠ ⊤} => ⟨stoppedValue X T.1, this T⟩)⟩

variable [NormedSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]

section Order

variable [PseudoMetrizableSpace ι] [BorelSpace ι] [Lattice E]
  [HasSolidNorm E] [IsOrderedAddMonoid E] [IsOrderedModule ℝ E] [IsFiniteMeasure P]

lemma _root_.MeasureTheory.Submartingale.classDL (hX1 : Submartingale X 𝓕 P)
    (hX2 : ∀ ω, RightContinuous (X · ω)) (hX3 : 0 ≤ X) :
    ClassDL X 𝓕 P := by
  refine ⟨Adapted.progMeasurable_of_rightContinuous hX1.1 hX2, fun t => ?_⟩
  have := (hX1.2.2 t).uniformIntegrable_condExp' (fun T :
    {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≤ t} => IsStoppingTime.measurableSpace_le T.2.1)
  refine uniformIntegrable_of_dominated this (fun T => ?_) (fun T => ⟨T, ?_⟩)
  · exact ((stronglyMeasurable_stoppedValue_of_le (hX1.1.progMeasurable_of_rightContinuous
      hX2) T.2.1 T.2.2).mono (𝓕.le' t)).aestronglyMeasurable
  · have : stoppedValue X T ≤ᵐ[P] P[stoppedValue X (fun ω => t)|T.2.1.measurableSpace] := by
      suffices lem : stoppedValue X ((fun ω => t) ⊓ T.1) ≤ᵐ[P]
        P[stoppedValue X (fun ω => t)|T.2.1.measurableSpace] from by
        have : T.1 ⊓ (fun ω => t) = T.1 := by simpa [inf_eq_left] using T.2.2
        simpa [inf_comm, this] using lem
      exact hX1.stoppedValue_min_ae_le_condExp 𝓕 hX2
        (Eventually.of_forall (fun ω => le_rfl)) T.2.1 (isStoppingTime_const 𝓕 t)
    simp only [stoppedValue_const] at this
    filter_upwards [this] with ω hω
    have p1 : 0 ≤ stoppedValue X T ω := by simpa [stoppedValue] using (hX3 (T.1 ω).untopA ω)
    have p2 := abs_of_nonneg (le_trans p1 hω)
    rw [← abs_of_nonneg p1, ← p2] at hω
    exact norm_le_norm_of_abs_le_abs hω

lemma _root_.MeasureTheory.Submartingale.uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime
    (hX1 : Submartingale X 𝓕 P) (hX2 : ∀ ω, RightContinuous (X · ω)) (hX3 : 0 ≤ X)
    (hX4 : UniformIntegrable X 1 P) :
    UniformIntegrable (fun T : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∃ t : ι, ∀ ω, T ω ≤ t}
      ↦ stoppedValue X T) 1 P := by
    have hcond := hX4.condExp' (fun T : {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∃ t : ι, ∀ ω, T ω ≤ t} =>
        IsStoppingTime.measurableSpace_le T.2.1)
    refine uniformIntegrable_of_dominated hcond (fun ⟨T, hT, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩ => ?_)
      (fun ⟨T, hT, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩ => ⟨⟨t, ⟨T, hT, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩⟩, ?_⟩)
    · exact ((stronglyMeasurable_stoppedValue_of_le (hX1.1.progMeasurable_of_rightContinuous
        hX2) hT ht).mono (𝓕.le' t)).aestronglyMeasurable
    · have : stoppedValue X T ≤ᵐ[P] P[stoppedValue X (fun ω => t)|hT.measurableSpace] := by
        suffices lem : stoppedValue X ((fun ω => t) ⊓ T) ≤ᵐ[P]
          P[stoppedValue X (fun ω => t)|hT.measurableSpace] from by
          have : T ⊓ (fun ω => t) = T := by simpa [inf_eq_left] using ht
          simpa [inf_comm, this] using lem
        exact hX1.stoppedValue_min_ae_le_condExp 𝓕 hX2
          (Eventually.of_forall (fun ω => le_rfl)) hT (isStoppingTime_const 𝓕 t)
      simp only [stoppedValue_const] at this
      filter_upwards [this] with ω hω
      apply norm_le_norm_of_abs_le_abs
      have p1 : 0 ≤ stoppedValue X T ω := by simpa [stoppedValue] using (hX3 (T ω).untopA ω)
      have p2 := abs_of_nonneg (le_trans p1 hω)
      rw [← abs_of_nonneg p1, ← p2] at hω
      exact hω

lemma _root_.MeasureTheory.Submartingale.classD_iff_uniformIntegrable
    (hX1 : Submartingale X 𝓕 P) (hX2 : ∀ ω, RightContinuous (X · ω)) (hX3 : 0 ≤ X) :
    ClassD X 𝓕 P ↔ UniformIntegrable X 1 P := by
  let S := {T | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≠ ⊤}
  let G (T : S) : (Ω → E) := stoppedValue X T.1
  refine ⟨fun hp => ?_, fun hq => ?_⟩
  · let constT (t : ι) : S := ⟨fun ω : Ω => t, ⟨isStoppingTime_const 𝓕 t, by simp⟩⟩
    have eq : X = G ∘ constT := by ext; simp [constT, G, stoppedValue]
    simpa [eq] using hp.2.comp constT
  · refine classD_of_uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime ?_ ?_
    · exact hX1.uniformIntegrable_bounded_stoppingTime hX2 hX3 hq
    · exact hX1.1.progMeasurable_of_rightContinuous hX2

end Order

lemma _root_.MeasureTheory.Martingale.classDL
    [IsFiniteMeasure P] [PseudoMetrizableSpace ι] [BorelSpace ι]
    (hX1 : Martingale X 𝓕 P) (hX2 : ∀ ω, RightContinuous (X · ω)) :
    ClassDL X 𝓕 P := by
  let Y := fun t ω ↦ ‖X t ω‖
  have hY_sub : Submartingale Y 𝓕 P := hX1.submartingale_convex_comp
    (convexOn_norm convex_univ) continuous_norm
    (fun t ↦ (hX1.integrable t).norm)
  have hY_cont : ∀ ω, RightContinuous (Y · ω) := fun ω t ↦ (hX2 ω t).norm
  have hY_nonneg : 0

by
```
Now we need to prove the equivalence. Let's prove both directions.

For the forward direction (if $X$ is of class D, then $X$ is uniformly integrable):
- Assume $X$ is of class D. Then $X$ is adapted and $\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a finite stopping time}\}$ is uniformly integrable.
- We need to show $X$ is uniformly integrable, i.e., for all $\epsilon > 0$, there exists a set $A$ with $\mathbb{P}(A) \geq 1-\epsilon$ such that $\sup_{t} \mathbb{E}[|X_t| \mathbf{1}_{A}] < \epsilon$.
- Since $\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a finite stopping time}\}$ is uniformly integrable, for any $\epsilon > 0$, there exists a set $A$ with $\mathbb{P}(A) \geq 1-\epsilon$ such that $\sup_{\tau} \mathbb{E}[|X_\tau| \mathbf{1}_{A}] < \epsilon$.
- For any $t$, we can choose a sequence of finite stopping times $\tau_n$ converging to $t$. By the uniform integrability, we can find a set $A$ such that $\sup_{\tau} \mathbb{E}[|X_\tau| \mathbf{1}_{A}] < \epsilon$. This implies $\sup_{t} \mathbb{E}[|X_t| \mathbf{1}_{A}] < \epsilon$ as well.

For the reverse direction (if $X$ is uniformly integrable, then $X$ is of class D):
- Assume $X$ is uniformly integrable. We need to show $X$ is of class D, i.e., $X$ is adapted and $\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a finite stopping time}\}$ is uniformly integrable.
- Adaptedness: This is given by the definition of a martingale, so it's straightforward.
- Uniform integrability: We need to show $\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a finite stopping time}\}$ is uniformly integrable. 
- For any $\epsilon > 0$, there exists a set $A$ with $\mathbb{P}(A) \geq 1-\epsilon$ such that $\sup_{t} \mathbb{E}[|X_t| \mathbf{1}_{A}] < \epsilon$.
- For any finite stopping time $\tau$, we have $X_\tau$ is uniformly integrable by the property of the supremum. More formally, for any $\epsilon > 0$, there exists a set $A$ with $\mathbb{P}(A) \geq 1-\epsilon$ such that $\sup_{t} \mathbb{E}[|X_t| \mathbf{1}_{A}] < \epsilon$ implies $\sup_{\tau} \mathbb{E}[|X_\tau| \mathbf{1}_{A}] < \epsilon$.

· -- Assume X is of class D, prove X is uniformly integrable

  rcases h with ⟨h1, h2⟩
  simpa using h2

namespace ProbabilityTheory

variable {ι Ω E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {P : Measure Ω}
  {X : ι → Ω → E}

/-- The condition that the running supremum process `(t, ω) ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖` is strongly
measurable as a function on the product. -/
def HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess [LinearOrder ι] [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E) : Prop :=
  (StronglyMeasurable (fun (tω : ι × Ω) ↦ ⨆ s ≤ tω.1, ‖X s ω‖ₑ))

/-- A stochastic process has integrable supremum if the function `(t, ω) ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖`
is strongly measurable and if for all `t`, the random variable `ω ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖`
is integrable. -/
def HasIntegrableSup [LinearOrder ι] [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E)
    (P : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop :=
  (HasStronglyMeasurableSupProcess (mΩ:= mΩ) X) ∧
     (∀ t, Integrable (fun ω ↦ ⨆ s ≤ t, ‖X s ω‖ₑ) P)

/-- A stochastic process has locally integrable supremum if it satisfies locally the property that
for all `t`, the random variable `ω ↦ sup_{s ≤ t} ‖X s ω‖` is integrable. -/
def HasLocallyIntegrableSup [LinearOrder ι] [OrderBot ι] [TopologicalSpace ι] [OrderTopology ι]
    [MeasurableSpace ι] (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ)
    (P : Measure Ω := by volume_tac) : Prop :=
  Locally (HasIntegrableSup · P) 𝓕 X P

  progMeasurable : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X
  uniformIntegrable : UniformIntegrable
    (fun (τ : {T : Ω → WithTop ι | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ ω, T ω ≠ ⊤}) ↦ stoppedValue X τ.1) 1 P

/-- A stochastic process $(X_t)$ is of class DL if it is adapted and for all $t$, the set
$\{X_\tau \mid \tau \text{ is a stopping time with } \tau \le t\}$ is uniformly integrable. -/
structure ClassDL (X : ι → Ω → E) (𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ) (P : Measure Ω) :
    Prop where
  progMeasurable : ProgMeasurable 𝓕 X
  uniformIntegrable (t : ι) : UniformIntegrable
    (fun (τ : {T : Ω → WithTop ι | IsStoppingTime 𝓕 T ∧ ∀ (ω : Ω), T ω ≤ t}) ↦ stoppedValue X τ.1) 1 P

lemma ClassD.classDL {𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ} {X : ι → Ω → E} (hX : ClassD X 𝓕 P) :
    ClassDL X 𝓕 P := by

The definition of Class D already requires that the set of stopped values is uniformly integrable. So, to prove that $X$ is uniformly integrable, we need to show that the set of values $\{X_t \mid t \in \mathbb{R}_+\}$ is uniformly integrable.

But the set of values $\{X_t \mid t \in \mathbb{R}_+\}$ is precisely the set of values that can be obtained by stopping the process $X$ at some time $t$. Therefore, the uniform integrability of the set of stopped values implies the uniform integrability of the entire process.

  uniformIntegrable : UniformIntegrable
